query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Diplomatist', 'A person tactful in negotiation', 'The essay highlights the diplomatist of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dirge', 'A funeral song or tune, expressing mourning for the dead', 'The essay highlights the dirge of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disabuse', 'To persuade someone that an idea or belief is mistaken', 'The author hoped the new policy would disabuse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disadvise', 'To advise against, to dissuade from', 'Her disadvise became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disaffection', 'A state of dissatisfaction with or alienation from something', 'Scholars noted a striking disaffection between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disagree', 'To not agree (harmonize)', 'Over time, the community learned to disagree its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disallow', 'To refuse to allow', 'Over time, the community learned to disallow its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disapprobation', 'An act or expression of condemnation or disapproval', 'The essay highlights the disapprobation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disapprove', 'To condemn, consider wrong or inappropriate', 'Critics argued that the plan might disapprove the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disarm', 'To take away the weapons of, to render defenceless', 'Critics argued that the plan might disarm the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disarrange', 'To undo the arrangement of, to disorder, to derange', 'The author hoped the new policy would disarrange tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disarray', 'To throw into disorder, to break the array of', 'Critics argued that the plan might disarray the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disavow', 'To disown, to repudiate, to refuse to acknowledge', 'The author hoped the new policy would disavow tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disavowal', 'A denial of relationship or responsibility towards something', 'The essay highlights the disavowal of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disband', 'Cause (an organized group) to break up', 'Critics argued that the plan might disband the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disbeliever', 'One who disbelieves, one who does not believe', 'The essay highlights the disbeliever of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disburden', 'To remove the load from a pack animal, unload a vehicle, etc', 'The author hoped the new policy would disburden tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disburse', 'To pay out, expend, usually from a public fund or treasury', 'Critics argued that the plan might disburse the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discard', 'To throw away, to reject', 'Over time, the community learned to discard its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discern', 'To perceive or recognize something', 'Critics argued that the plan might discern the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discernible', 'Noticeable or able to be perceived', '“There was a discernible improvement in his work.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disciple', 'A person who is a pupil of the doctrines of another', 'Scholars noted a striking disciple between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disciplinary', 'Corrective, administering or used for discipline', 'Scholars noted a striking disciplinary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discipline', 'To train someone by instruction and practice', 'Over time, the community learned to discipline its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disclaim', 'To renounce all claim to, to deny responsibility for', 'Over time, the community learned to disclaim its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disclose', 'To make secret information known', 'Critics argued that the plan might disclose the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discolor', 'To change or lose color', 'Over time, the community learned to discolor its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discombobulate', 'To disconcert or confuse (someone)', 'The author hoped the new policy would discombobulate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discomfit', 'To defeat completely, to rout', 'Critics argued that the plan might discomfit the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discomfort', 'To cause annoyance or distress', 'The author hoped the new policy would discomfort tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disconcert', 'To disturb the composure of, to unsettle', 'Critics argued that the plan might disconcert the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disconnect', 'To sever or interrupt a connection', 'The author hoped the new policy would disconnect tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disconsolate', 'Cheerless, dreary', 'Critics argued that the plan might disconsolate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discontinuance', 'The occurrence of something being terminated, a cessation', 'Scholars noted a striking discontinuance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discord', 'Disagreement between people', 'Scholars noted a striking discord between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discordancy', 'The state of being at variance or in disagreement', 'Her discordancy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discordant', 'Lacking harmony or agreement', '“Their discordant opinions led to heated arguments.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discount', 'A reduction in the usual price of something', 'The essay highlights the discount of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discountenance', 'To have an unfavorable opinion of, to disapprove', 'The author hoped the new policy would discountenance tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discourse', 'To engage in discussion or conversation, to converse', 'Over time, the community learned to discourse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discourteous', 'Showing rudeness and a lack of consideration for others', 'The reviewer described the tone as discourteous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discover', 'To remove the cover from, to uncover', 'The author hoped the new policy would discover tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discredit', 'To harm the good reputation of a person, to defame', 'Over time, the community learned to discredit its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discreet', 'Respectful of privacy or secrecy, quiet, diplomatic', 'Her discreet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discrepancy', 'A difference or inconsistency', '“There was a discrepancy between the two reports.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discrepant', 'Inconsistent; conflicting; at variance', 'Her discrepant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discrete', 'Individually separate and distinct', 'The reviewer described the tone as discrete, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discretion', 'The ability to make responsible decisions or keep something confidential', '“Use discretion when discussing sensitive matters.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discriminate', 'To recognize a distinction, to differentiate', 'Critics argued that the plan might discriminate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discursive', 'Digressing from subject to subject', 'The author hoped the new policy would discursive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
